title: bidens vulgata f. puberula
intro: Bidens vulgata f. puberula, also known as the hairy beggar-ticks, is a plant species within the Bidens genus of flowering plants, characterized by its hairy leaves and stems. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. Its medicinal properties offer various health benefits, including reducing fever, soothing skin irritations, and aiding in wound healing. Bidens vulgata f. puberula can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or salves for topical application, making it a valuable addition to natural remedies.
